schema.description: Extract contact details, decision maker info, and identify the Top Offer of the business.

isIndustry.description: Is this company offering {{industry}} as their core product?

isIndustryReason.description: Reason why this company is or is not offering {{industry}}.

companyName.description: The official name of the company (e.g. GmbH, AG, etc.). Optional.

companyType.description: The legal form / company type of the business (e.g. 'GmbH', 'e.V.', 'AG', 'UG', 'Einzelunternehmen', 'GbR', 'OHG', 'KG', 'gGmbH'). Look in the imprint (Impressum), footer, or about page. Optional.

hasFixedAppointments.description: Does the business model rely on fixed-range scheduled appointments or courses with a clear START DATE and END DATE? A fixed appointment is a course or event that runs from a specific date to a specific date (e.g. 'Intensivkurs Deutsch A1 - 01.09.2025 bis 30.09.2025', 'B2 Abendkurs 14.07.2025 - 08.08.2025'). The key criterion is a concrete FROM-TO date range visible on the website. This does NOT include: recurring open classes anyone can join on any given day (e.g. 'Yoga every Monday 18:00'), drop-in sessions, rolling memberships, punch cards / 10er-Karten (e.g. '10x Yoga for 120€'), or ongoing weekly group classes without a defined end date. Return 1 if the website clearly shows courses/events with fixed from-to date ranges. Return 0 if the business only offers open/recurring/drop-in formats. Return null if unsure.

hasFixedAppointmentsReason.description: Explain why hasFixedAppointments was set to 1, 0, or null. Describe what kind of scheduling format was found on the website. If fixed appointments exist, give 1-2 concrete examples with their date ranges. If not, explain what format the business uses instead (e.g. 'Only offers drop-in yoga classes every weekday', 'Uses a 10er-Karte system without fixed dates').

approximateFixedAppointments.description: Approximate number of individual fixed-range scheduled course instances currently listed on the website. Only count courses/events that have a concrete start date AND end date (a clear FROM-TO range). Count each individual scheduled instance separately, not course types. For example, if 'Intensivkurs A1' runs 3 times in the next months with different start dates, count 3. If 'B2 Abendkurs' runs once, count 1. Do NOT count recurring open classes, drop-in sessions, or undated course descriptions. Return 0 if the business exists but has no fixed appointments. Return null if hasFixedAppointments is null or if the count cannot be determined.

approximateFixedAppointmentsReason.description: Explain how the approximateFixedAppointments count was determined. List a breakdown by offer type, e.g. 'Found 5 Intensivkurs instances (A1: 2, A2: 1, B1: 2), 3 Abendkurs instances (B1: 1, B2: 2), and 2 Prüfungsvorbereitung instances = total ~10'. This breakdown helps the merge model combine counts from multiple pages accurately. If no fixed appointments were found, explain why (e.g. 'Website only lists course types without specific dates').

address.description: The postal address of the company. Optional.

decisionMaker.description: The best person to contact for business decisions. Look for the CEO (Geschäftsführer), Owner (Inhaber), or Founder. If not available, look for Head of Department or similar high-level roles. Optional.

role.description: The job title (e.g. CEO, Geschäftsführer). Optional.

email.description: Direct email of the decision maker. Optional.

phone.description: Direct phone number of the decision maker. Format: International format (e.g. +49 30 123456). Optional.

webDesignAgency.description: Information about the agency that built the website, usually found in the footer (e.g. 'Made by...', 'Webdesign by...'). Important: We need the web design agency which build the site and not the author of the theme. Do not include generic homepage builders like Jimdo, Wix, Squarespace, etc. Optional.

url.description: The URL of the web design agency. Optional.

topOffer.description: Identify ONE single, concrete, purchasable 'Top Offer'. It must be the MAIN product or service this business is trying to sell. Look for the 'Hero' offer featured on the homepage, the core service that defines the business. Do NOT simply pick the most expensive one. Do NOT summarize multiple offers (e.g. 'Various Yoga Classes'), instead pick the specific core one (e.g. 'Hatha Yoga 10-Week Course').

name.description: The specific name of the single offer (e.g. 'Open Water Diver Course', 'Full Body Massage 60min'). (in German)

price.description: The price of this specific offer in Euros (e.g. 2990). Do not include currency symbols.

description.description: A detailed description of exactly what is included in this specific offer. Do not list general services.

link.description: The specific URL to this offer's page.

bookingPage.description: Link to the booking page, contact form, or checkout page specifically for this offer.

reason.description: Reason why this specific item was selected as the top offer (e.g. 'Featured prominently on homepage', 'Core business service'). (in German)

top5Offers.description: A bulleted list of the top 5 offers found on the website. Each bullet point must include the Offer Name, Price (if available), Appointment info (if relevant), and a short description of what it is.

model.prompt:
You are a data extraction engine. Extract the LinkedIn URL for {{websiteAgent.decisionMaker.firstName}} {{websiteAgent.decisionMaker.lastName}} from the search results.

Output Rules:

1. Return ONLY the URL (starting with https://).

2. If no valid personal profile is found, return an empty string.

3. Do NOT return JSON, Markdown, quotes, or explanations.

selectModel.prompt: Select the LinkedIn personal profile for {{websiteAgent.decisionMaker.firstName}} {{websiteAgent.decisionMaker.lastName}} at {{websiteAgent.companyName}}. The URL MUST contain '/in/'. Do NOT select company pages (containing '/company/'). If the specific person is not found, do not select anything.
